title: Zoto Eval Analyser Payload
description: Strict JSON shape returned by the LLM-driven primitive analyser subagent (subtask 04). The analyser receives a single primitive (skill/command/agent/hook/rule) and must respond with this object — and ONLY this object — so the caller can deterministically map it onto generated eval cases. Every field has a documented intent block so future maintainers (and the LLM itself, when this prompt is regenerated) can stay aligned with the contract.

schema_version.description: Hard-pinned schema version. Bumped when the analyser payload shape changes in a non-backward-compatible way; consumers MUST refuse payloads with a value other than 1.

analyser_version.description: Free-form version string baked into the cache key (alongside `model_id` and `source_hash`). The analyser script publishes a single canonical value; bumping it forces every cached payload to invalidate on the next run, which is the standard knob for changes to the analyser prompt or post-processing logic.

model_id.description: Concrete Cursor model id used to generate this payload (e.g. `composer-2`, `opus-4.6`, `sonnet`). Mirrors the `_meta.primitive_analysis.model_id` callers use to detect model swaps.

target_id.description: Canonical target id (`<kind>:<name>`). MUST match the `kind` field below — consumers cross-validate the prefix against `kind` and reject mismatches.

kind.description: Primitive kind. Drives which template (subtasks 06-10) consumes this payload. The analyser MUST tailor prompt style and assertion vocabulary to the kind (commands speak `/cmd …`, agents speak natural English requests, hooks speak about phase events, etc.).

source_path.description: POSIX repo-relative path to the source markdown (e.g. `plugins/zoto-eval-system/skills/zoto-create-evals/SKILL.md`). Backslashes are forbidden; callers normalise before validation.

source_hash.description: sha256 of the cache-key tuple `(normalised source content + analyser_version + model_id)`. Bumping the analyser version OR swapping the model invalidates every previous cache entry, which is the intended behaviour.

summary.description: Short natural-language summary of the analyser's findings — one or two sentences. Stored verbatim in `_meta.primitive_analysis.summary` and used by the updater to compose differential prompts on drift.

cases.description: At least one realistic case per primitive. Each case maps 1:1 onto a stamped eval row in the relevant backend template (subtasks 06-10).

scenario.description: Short label (≤120 chars) describing the scenario this case exercises (e.g. `happy path — operator approves all defaults`). Used as the case `id` slug source by downstream stampers.

prompt.description: Realistic, copy-pastable user/agent message — what a real human or upstream agent would actually type. For commands: `/<cmd-name> <realistic args>`. For agents: a natural-language request that triggers the documented flow. For skills: an upstream agent message that would cause the skill to load. For hooks: a description of the triggering Cursor lifecycle event. The analyser MUST NOT emit placeholders like `<replace me>`, `your prompt here`, `<command>`, etc.

follow_ups.description: Optional ordered list of natural follow-up turns that exercise multi-turn flows (`askQuestion` answers, refinement prompts, etc.). Omit when the scenario is single-turn. Same realism constraints as `prompt` apply.

fixtures.description: Per-case fixture overlay applied on top of the repo-wide baseline (subtask 05). Emit ONLY when the analyser determines the case requires per-case workspace state beyond the baseline. When omitted (or when `files: []`), the stamper writes `fixtures.files: []` and refuses to invent overlays elsewhere.

path.description: Sandbox-relative POSIX path (e.g. `workspace/.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`). Forbidden: absolute paths, `..` segments, env-var expansions, repo-specific identifiers (operator usernames, host names, secrets). Stampers reject violating paths.

content.description: Inline file contents. Mutually exclusive with `from`; one of the two MUST be present. Forbidden: real env-var values, secrets, machine identifiers.

from.description: Repo-relative path to copy from at stamp time. Mutually exclusive with `content`. Forbidden: paths that escape the repo root.

fixture_justifications.description: Free-form rationale lines, one per file in `fixtures.files[]`, explaining why each overlay is required. The stamper preserves this list inside `_meta.primitive_analysis.fixture_justifications` so reviewers can spot fixtures that were added without justification.

expected_filesystem.description: Optional declarative filesystem expectation evaluated by the runner's per-case sandbox snapshot diff. Use sparingly — prefer behavioural assertions over byte-exact filesystem checks.

expected_output.description: Short prose description of what the agent run should produce. Used by the LLM-judge graders as the canonical reference. Plain English; no JSON envelopes, no placeholder vocabulary.

assertions.description: Behaviour-level assertions describing concrete checks against the documented intent. At least one assertion per documented capability of the primitive. Forbidden: vague claims like `behaved correctly`, `worked as expected`, `followed documented sequencing`. Required: concrete, falsifiable statements (e.g. `manifest.yml records at least one target with kind: skill`).
